research.title: Observability for Next.js: OpenTelemetry, Traces, and the Serverless Blind Spot
research.whyTrending: The React2Shell post-mortem highlighted that you can't image a serverless function — observability is the answer and it's uncovered. OpenTelemetry + Next.js instrumentation has strong ops search intent.

seo.suggestedTitle: Observability for Next.js: OpenTelemetry Done Right

gaps: Setup guides abound but few explain the serverless BLIND SPOT (why you need this: no disk to image, no full-request trace)
@vercel/otel vs manual NodeSDK tradeoff (edge runtime) glossed
Correlating logs to traces (trace_id in structured logs) rarely shown
Cold-start / lazy-init gotcha (10s startup limit) buried
What custom spans are actually worth adding (DB, external API, server action)
strengths: Next.js docs canonical on instrumentation hook

officialSources.note: structured JSON logs for Vercel Functions

expertInsights: instrumentation.ts at project ROOT (not app/ or pages/), export register(); auto-detected Next 15+
Two approaches: @vercel/otel (zero-config, edge-compatible) vs manual NodeSDK + OTLPTraceExporter
Serverless blind spot: Vercel gives function-level timings but NOT end-to-end traces across middleware/DB/3rd-party
OTel is vendor-agnostic: OTLP to any backend (SigNoz, Datadog, Axiom, Honeycomb)
Custom spans via trace.getTracer(); set attributes + SpanStatusCode
Cold-start limit: if init > ~10s, load instrumentation lazily; edge runtime requires @vercel/otel
Correlate logs to traces by injecting trace_id/span_id into structured JSON logs
Log Drains export to external platforms
commonProblems: instrumentation.ts in wrong folder -> silently no traces
Using manual NodeSDK on edge runtime (unsupported) instead of @vercel/otel
Logs and traces not correlated (no trace_id) -> cant pivot
Heavy init blowing the cold-start budget

strategy.angle: Frame observability as the answer to the serverless blind spot the React2Shell post-mortem exposed (you cant image an ephemeral function) - then give the correct setup (instrumentation.ts + @vercel/otel vs manual, and when each), the custom spans actually worth adding, and the log-trace correlation that turns three signals into one debuggable story
strategy.title: Observability for Next.js: OpenTelemetry and the Serverless Blind Spot
strategy.excerpt: You cannot SSH into a serverless function that already scaled to zero. Observability is how you see inside it anyway. Heres OpenTelemetry in Next.js done right - instrumentation.ts, the @vercel/otel-vs-manual choice, the custom spans worth adding, and correlating logs to traces.
hooks: Contrarian: the reason to add OpenTelemetry isnt dashboards, its that your production server no longer exists by the time you go to debug it
Gotcha: put instrumentation.ts one folder too deep and you get zero traces, silently
Practical: Vercel shows you function timings but not the trace across middleware, DB, and third-party calls - thats the gap OTel fills
outline: Hook: debugging a server that no longer exists
What OpenTelemetry gives you: traces, spans, logs
Setup: instrumentation.ts and the register hook
@vercel/otel vs manual NodeSDK - which and when
The custom spans actually worth adding
